name: Imposter Layout Intent
summary: A primitive layout region-role (#1680 taxonomy): composition-intent = center a child over a positioning context (overlay). Identity is the arrangement, not the CSS — FUI ships one block per role and the mechanism (absolute positioning / a single-cell grid stack) is an impl seam. Project-less; per-project styling rides presentational variants + plateau assembler presets.

fixed.description: Whether the child is positioned relative to the viewport (fixed) or the nearest positioned ancestor. Default false (ancestor).

margin.description: Minimum breathing room kept between the overlaid child and the container edges. Absent → no minimum.

contain.description: Whether the child is clipped to the container's bounds. Default false.

description:
What This Is

Centers a child over a positioning context, breaking out of the document flow to overlay it — a modal-positioned panel, a centered loading state, an overlay badge.

Composition-intent (the identity)

A primitive layout region-role (layoutTier: region-role), one of the per-role taxonomy ratified in #1680. Its identity is the composition-intent — center a child over a positioning context (overlay) — impl-agnostic. FUI ships exactly one block for this role; the CSS mechanism (absolute positioning / a single-cell grid stack) is an impl seam, never the role's identity (platform-decisions.md#layout-role-composition-intent).

Dimensions (UX-only)

  • fixed: Whether the child is positioned relative to the viewport (fixed) or the nearest positioned ancestor. Default false (ancestor) (true · false).
  • margin: Minimum breathing room kept between the overlaid child and the container edges. Absent → no minimum (none · xs · sm · md · lg).
  • contain: Whether the child is clipped to the container's bounds. Default false (true · false).

Annotation, not identity

Distinct from the modal / anchor behavior intents — imposter is the arrangement; dialog/popover semantics are an optional annotation (#1680 Fork-1a: behavior can-do vs layout is-a).
